name: Blackberries
why_now: Wild and cultivated blackberries both peak in July in Southern California. They are deeply flavored, slightly tart, and good with almost nothing added.
how_to_choose: Deep, uniform black with no red druplets remaining
Check the bottom of the container for mold
A little give without being mushy
buy_this_much: One pint. Use within two or three days.
price_note: July is peak season — pints that cost $4–5 off-season often drop to $2–3. One of the best deals of the summer.

flavor_paths.name: Breakfast
flavor_paths.description: Over Greek yogurt with mint and honey.

flavor_paths.name: Dessert
flavor_paths.description: With a little cream or over good vanilla ice cream.

flavor_paths.name: Salad
flavor_paths.description: In a green salad with peaches and a light vinaigrette.

weekday_uses: Greek yogurt topping in the morning
Added to a fruit plate with peaches and mint
weekend_use: Fruit plate with peaches, mint, and honey as a light dessert.
storage: Refrigerate unwashed and use within two to three days. Wash only just before eating.
one_thing_to_learn: Blackberries are fragile. They do not improve with time. Buy them close to when you will eat them and treat the container gently.
market_question: How ripe are these right now?
